description: The reflexive instance: the paper produced under the strategy it describes, AND the monitor over the four object instances A-D (its verifier re-derives each ledgered claim — including claims about A-D's results — from the hard ground truth, never from the writer's prose). Dominant coherent-and-wrong: a manuscript whose prose reads as faithful to the literature while a reported number does not recompute or overclaims a result. The four sub_loop bridge rows link down to instance-a..d; their returned results are the mediated channel the paper re-derives from. The reflexive soft-side claim (selfhood / faithful reading of the literature) has no executable refuter and is closed at the human/external reviewer terminus.

sources.name: Read literature (archived reading records)
sources.notes: bib/records/*; every cite has an archived read record

sources.name: Paper-spec (paper-impl.md / paper-spec)
sources.notes: the authored normative ceiling for the manuscript

sources.name: Raw repository ground truth
sources.notes: the real src/A..D outputs, eval/ and verify/ artifacts the harness must faithfully recompute from

sources.name: Recompute + build harness (the hard lower bound)
sources.notes: every number recomputes from a generator, manuscript compiles, every cite resolves

sources.name: Claim ledger (claims/ledger.tsv)
sources.notes: the writer's ledger of CITE/RESULT claims

sources.name: Verifier's independent re-derivation
sources.notes: each ledgered claim re-derived from source/artifact, never from the writer's prose

sources.name: Instance A returned results
sources.notes: mediated returned outputs of instance-a (sub_loop bridge)

sources.name: External / human reviewer judgment
sources.notes: closes the reflexive soft side; no executable refuter

actors.name: Main-thread coordinator (the only judge)
actors.builds: approvals, gate verdicts, the claim-ledger reconciliation, and the loop's SL-1.0 instance-paper.sl.json

upper_bound.text: the read literature and the paper-spec it judges the bridge against

lower_bound.text: the recompute/build harness and the bands' artifacts it reconciles (writer ledger vs verifier re-derivation vs instance results)

forbidden_moves: edit the manuscript or the verifier's evidence
accept an item whose bridge it has not editorially judged

actors.name: Literature agent (soft upper-bound guard)
actors.builds: the paper-spec and the read-before-cite guard over the literature

upper_bound.text: the read literature it transcribes and guards

lower_bound.text: expressibility: every cite must resolve to bib AND an archived read record when the harness runs

forbidden_moves: read or anchor to the writer's manuscript
let a cite stand without a read record

actors.name: Writer agent (the deliverable)
actors.builds: the manuscript and the claim ledger

upper_bound.text: faithful to the paper-spec and the read literature (claims no more than they license)

lower_bound.text: consistent with the hard truth: every number recomputes and the manuscript builds when the harness runs

actors.name: Verifier agent (independent re-derivation)
actors.builds: the independent re-derivation of every ledgered claim

upper_bound.text: the paper-spec's required claims and the literature, re-derived independently

lower_bound.text: each claim re-derived from the hard ground truth and the instances' returned results — never the writer's prose

forbidden_moves: read the writer's manuscript or ledger
derive a claim from the prose instead of the source

actors.name: Bench agent (hard lower-bound builder)
actors.builds: the recompute + build harness that pins every number to its generator

upper_bound.text: the single claim under test: each reported number recomputes deterministically

lower_bound.text: the raw repository ground truth the harness must faithfully recompute from

forbidden_moves: implement a fix in the manuscript
build the harness to match the writer's claimed numbers

actors.name: Bridge -> instance-a
actors.builds: instance-a collapsed to one row; returns its machine-verdicted results
forbidden_moves: expose instance-a's internal sources directly to the monitor

actors.name: Bridge -> instance-b
actors.builds: instance-b collapsed to one row; returns its machine-verdicted results
forbidden_moves: expose instance-b's internal sources directly to the monitor

actors.name: Bridge -> instance-c
actors.builds: instance-c collapsed to one row; returns its machine-verdicted results
forbidden_moves: expose instance-c's internal sources directly to the monitor

actors.name: Bridge -> instance-d
actors.builds: instance-d collapsed to one row; returns its machine-verdicted results
forbidden_moves: expose instance-d's internal sources directly to the monitor

actors.name: External / human reviewer (terminus)
actors.builds: the terminal editorial judgment that closes the reflexive soft side

upper_bound.text: the paper-spec and literature: is the reflexive reading faithful?

lower_bound.text: no executable oracle — the reflexive selfhood claim has no hard refuter; this absence is why the reviewer is the terminus
lower_bound.absent_reason: the soft-side faithfulness/selfhood claim is not mechanically decidable; closed by a disjoint human reviewer, not a machine

forbidden_moves: claim the automation alone closed the soft side

disjointness.rationale: The reflexive cast holds disjoint bases — lit holds the literature (never the manuscript), writer holds the spec+literature and the build (never the verifier's evidence), verifier re-derives from the hard truth and the instances' returned results (never the writer's prose), bench pins the hard truth from raw repo data (never the writer's claims). The four sub_loop bridges link to A-D by their returned results, NOT their internal sources, so the monitor never bounds itself on an instance-internal artifact. The coordinator's editorial judgment — the one soft-side hole — is closed at the human/external reviewer terminus, by disjoint authority, not mechanically.
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: the verifier (and bench) never see the writer's manuscript/ledger and re-derive independently; the monitor reads only the instances' returned results via the bridges, never their internals

catchability.blind_spot: its own un-squeezed editorial judgment of whether the paper faithfully reads the literature
catchability.via: external/human review — the terminus that closes the soft side

catchability.blind_spot: an over-broad reading: a cite that does not actually support the claim
catchability.via: independent re-derivation requires each claim's source to actually support it

catchability.blind_spot: coherent-and-wrong prose: faithful-looking text whose number does not recompute or overclaims
catchability.via: re-derivation from the hard ground truth; Gate B recompute mismatch

catchability.blind_spot: a re-derivation that silently skips a ledgered claim
a harness that computes a consistent-but-wrong number not matching raw data
catchability.via: the verifier re-derives from raw source independently of the harness build
the verifier re-derives A's claimed result from A's returned ground-truth outputs

disjointness.terminus: human_reviewer (external/human review closes the reflexive soft side; the automation alone does not)
